mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) directly reflects robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month increases fail to predict positive S&P 500 20-day returns with Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25 percent, the zero lower bound constraint forces investors to bid up equity valuations as Treasury yields offer no income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive compared to periods above 0.25% (Welch's t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal a tightening labor market that accelerates wage-driven consumption and earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series move together.
failure_condition: The joint signal of PAYEMS increasing and UNRATE decreasing produces a 20-day forward return Rank IC below 0.03, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) relative to individual series.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, month-over-month industrial production growth (INDPRO) signals capacity utilization recovery and margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; this relationship vanishes when labor markets are tight.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, month-over-month INDPRO increases fail to coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal's Rank IC drops below 0.03 in that regime.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) falling below 600 thousand units signals a collapse in residential investment and construction employment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 600k are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered observations over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a near-zero federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) generate negative real interest rates that force capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as financial repression lifts risk assets.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, the interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS yields a 60-day forward return Rank IC below 0.03, or the spread between CPI and FEDFUNDS fails to correlate positively with returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing output and supply-chain stress, which compresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A jump in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 8.5 percent triggers forced household deleveraging and collapse in aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as recession risk is priced in.
failure_condition: UNRATE levels exceeding 8.5% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of instances over any rolling 3-year period.
